hero.title:
Built for speed.
Designed for scale.
hero.subtitle: Discover how Alpis empowers IT teams to resolve issues faster, automate the mundane, and deliver exceptional support across the entire organization.

omnichannel.title: Omnichannel Ticketing
omnichannel.description: One unified inbox for every single request.
features: Ingest tickets from email, web portal, API, and chat integrations.
Automatic thread parsing for pristine conversation views.
Smart collision detection prevents multiple agents replying at once.
Custom ticket forms with conditional logic for the portal.

automation.title: Intelligent Automation
automation.description: Put your most common workflows on autopilot.
features: Auto-assign tickets based on workload, skill set, or department.
One-click macro execution for common responses.
Automated SLA breach escalations via SMS or PagerDuty.

security.title: Enterprise Security
security.description: Bank-grade security built directly into the database.
features: Row-Level Security (RLS) ensures absolute tenant data isolation.
Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) for granular viewing permissions.
Comprehensive audit logs tracking every state mutation.

analytics.title: Advanced Analytics
analytics.description: Insights that drive your team's performance.
features: Real-time dashboards for queue health and agent performance.
Custom report builder with CSV/PDF exports.
Deep dive into SLA achievement rates and resolution bottlenecks.
CSAT tracking and automated feedback collection.

cta.title: Ready to transform your service desk?
cta.subtitle: Join hundreds of forward-thinking IT teams already using Alpis.

hero.title: Pricing that scales with you
hero.subtitle: Start for free, upgrade when you need advanced features. No hidden fees, no per-ticket overages.

starter.name: Starter
starter.tagline: Perfect for small teams and startups.

pro.name: Pro
pro.tagline: For growing IT teams needing automation.

items.q: Do I need a credit card to sign up?
items.a: No! You can sign up and use our Starter plan entirely for free without entering any payment information. You only need a card if you decide to upgrade to Pro.

items.q: How does the 'per 5 agents' pricing work?
items.a: Our Pro plan is billed in blocks of 5 agents. If you have 1-5 agents, it's $49/mo. For 6-10 agents, it's $98/mo. This simplifies billing and avoids micromanaging licenses.
Yes, self-hosting is available on our Enterprise plan. We provide Docker images and Helm charts for deployment on your own infrastructure. Contact sales for details.
A mailbox is an email address you connect via IMAP/SMTP (e.g., support@yourcompany.com). The Starter plan includes 1 mailbox, Pro includes 10.

hero.title: Security by Design
hero.subtitle: Security isn't an afterthought at Alpis. It's woven into the fabric of our architecture, ensuring your data remains isolated, encrypted, and compliant.

dbIsolation.title: Database-Level Isolation
dbIsolation.p1: Unlike legacy SaaS platforms that rely on flawed application-level filtering, Alpis leverages PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS).
dbIsolation.p2: Every query is cryptographically scoped to the current tenant at the database engine level. A bug in application code cannot leak data.

encryption.title: End-to-End Encryption
encryption.p1: In Transit: All data transmitted between your browser and our API is encrypted using TLS 1.3 with strict HSTS policies enforced.
encryption.p2: At Rest: Databases, automated backups, and file storage volumes are encrypted at rest using industry-standard AES-256 encryption.

auth.title: Authentication & Access
items: SSO via SAML and OpenID Connect (Enterprise)

infra.title: Infrastructure & Compliance
infra.p1: Alpis is hosted on SOC 2 compliant infrastructure (AWS). We conduct regular penetration testing and vulnerability scanning.

disclosure.title: Vulnerability Disclosure
disclosure.subtitle: We believe in working closely with the security research community. If you've found a vulnerability, please disclose it responsibly.

hero.title: Get in touch
hero.subtitle: Whether you have questions about pricing, features, or need help setting up a custom integration, our team is here for you.

sales.title: Chat with sales
sales.subtitle: Speak directly to our sales team about Enterprise plans.

support.title: Customer support
support.subtitle: Need technical assistance with your instance?

hero.subtitle: Multi-tenant ticketing, email ingestion, SLA enforcement, and real-time notifications - in one platform for MSPs and IT teams.

features.title: Unified ticket engine
features.body: Email, portal, and API - all channels flow into one queue. Status, priority, SLA, assignee, tags, and full history on every ticket.

features.title: Row-level security
features.body: Postgres RLS enforces tenant isolation at the database layer. No application-level filtering that can be bypassed.

features.title: IMAP/SMTP mailboxes
features.body: Connect unlimited mailboxes per org. STARTTLS, OAuth, RFC-compliant threading.

features.title: SLA policies
features.body: Define response and resolution targets per priority. Breach alerts auto-escalate.

features.title: RBAC + ABAC
features.body: Four system roles with granular permissions. ABAC scoping limits what agents can see.

features.title: Channel notifications
features.body: In-app, email, push, and SMS fan-out. Per-user preferences per event type.

channels.heading: Every channel, one queue.
channels.subtitle: Tickets arrive from email, web portal, API, and - soon - WhatsApp and Telegram. The engine doesn't care about source. Your agents get one unified view.

security.heading:
Tenant isolation
enforced at the DB.
security.body: Postgres Row Level Security policies run at the database layer. No tenant data ever leaks through a missed WHERE clause in application code.
items: Every org gets its own RLS context via SET LOCAL GUC
Background workers use bypass_rls=on super-admin context
Rotating JWT refresh tokens, SHA-256 hashed in DB
Full audit log on every state change

pricing.heading: Simple, transparent pricing.
pricing.subtitle: No per-ticket fees. No surprise overage charges.

plans.name: Pro
plans.desc: For growing IT teams and MSPs.

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.desc: For large organizations with compliance needs.

cta.heading: Ready to modernize your service desk?
cta.subtitle: Set up in minutes. No infrastructure to manage.

hero.title: Changelog
hero.subtitle: Stay up to date with the latest features, improvements, and bug fixes in Alpis.

releases.title: Advanced SLAs and Custom Business Hours
releases.description: This release focuses on giving enterprise teams more control over their Service Level Agreements. You can now define custom business hours per department and set multi-tier escalation policies.

changes.text: Added support for custom business hours in SLA policies.
Introduced multi-tier escalation rules for SLA breaches.
Optimized ticket list rendering for queues with >10,000 active tickets.
Fixed a bug with email thread reply parsing on Outlook clients.

releases.title: ABAC Release & Webhooks
releases.description: Granular control is here. We've rolled out Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), allowing you to restrict agent visibility based on department, tag, or custom attributes.

changes.text: Added outgoing Webhook integrations for ticket lifecycle events.
Redesigned the macro execution menu for faster access.
Fixed an issue where CC'd users weren't receiving initial notifications.

releases.title: Public Beta Launch
releases.description: The initial public release of Alpis Service Desk. A massive thank you to our early alpha testers for their invaluable feedback over the last six months.

changes.text: Core ticketing engine with email and portal ingestion.
Tenant isolation via Postgres Row-Level Security.
Basic SLA management and RBAC roles.

content.title: Introduction to Alpis
content.intro: Alpis is a high-performance, developer-friendly IT service desk. This documentation covers everything from initial setup to advanced API integrations.

quickstart.title: Quickstart Guide
quickstart.subtitle: Get your service desk running in under 5 minutes.

api.title: API Reference
api.subtitle: Interact programmatically with tickets and users.

content.architectureBody: Alpis is built on a multi-tenant PostgreSQL database using Row-Level Security (RLS) to guarantee data isolation. The backend leverages Node.js with tRPC for end-to-end type safety, while the frontend is a React SPA powered by Vite and TanStack Router.
content.helpBody: If you get stuck, check out our <communityLink>Community Forums</communityLink> or reach out to <contactLink>Support</contactLink>. Enterprise customers have access to a dedicated Slack channel and a Technical Account Manager.
